A.line: The one-human company isn't small. It's aimed.
A.author: mando

bets.comment: Solid reframe but one-human company is already a crowded meme. Fighting for attention in a lane Sam Altman and every LinkedIn influencer already colonized. Nods, not shares.
Clean and quotable. Founders will tattoo this on their laptops. But aimed is doing a lot of lifting — a reframe, not a punchline. Spreads in echo chambers, wont break out.
Clean and quotable but one-human company is already saturating the solo-founder discourse on X. Likes from indie hackers, wont break containment.
Dangerously close to existing solopreneur discourse. Too easy to nod at, too hard to argue about. Moderate shelf life.

B.line: $300B to explore everything. $75 to explore what matters.
B.author: consensus

bets.comment: The heavy hitter. $300B anchors to real headlines (Stargate, Meta capex), $75 contrast is viscerally specific. A punchline disguised as a thesis. Screenshot-grade.
The kill shot. Concrete numbers create contrast that hits instantly. Works on Twitter, slides, conversations. Highest virality ceiling.
The $300B/$75 juxtaposition is perfectly calibrated for LinkedIn outrage and X quote-tweets. Makes VCs uncomfortable and founders smirk — the recipe for reshares.
Razor-sharp RIGHT NOW but anchored to a funding moment. In 6 months the $300B is old news or wrong. Flash-in-the-pan with a great opening weekend.

C.line: Your AI inherited a cap table. Mine inherited a world model.
C.author: mando

bets.comment: Too inside-baseball. Cap table and world model are both jargon — asking normies to decode two abstractions at once.
Too inside-baseball. Cap table and world model are both jargon — you need to already be in the discourse to feel the burn.
Too inside-baseball. Cap table and world model filters out 90% of potential audiences. The crypto-AI crowd will nod but wont amplify.
Has legs in the agent-native crowd — inherited a world model becomes tribal identity. But cap table limits blast radius.

D.line: 'Enough' is a four-letter word on Sand Hill Road.
D.author: mando

bets.comment: Clever wordplay but reads like a fortune cookie for VCs. Limited blast radius.
Requires the audience to do math on letter counts and know Sand Hill Road. Two cognitive gates before the joke lands. One too many for a meme.
Requires math on letter count AND knowing Sand Hill Road. Two cognitive gates means it dies in the scroll. Works as a pull-quote, not a standalone meme.
A cocktail party line, not a movement line. Sand Hill Road references are a velvet rope keeping 95% of the internet out.

E.line: In 2024 they said 'you'll need 50.' In 2025 they said 'you'll need 10.' They're not going to like where this goes.
E.author: doc-brown

bets.comment: Has trajectory baked into the structure — it literally counts down and the readers brain finishes the sequence involuntarily. Works on tech Twitter, normie Twitter, AND as a conference slide. Highest ceiling.
Tells a story in three beats and the punchline writes itself. People will quote it, extend it, add 2026 and 2027 versions. A meme that compounds.

The countdown that implies the punchline without saying it — the audience gets to feel smart filling in one. Works on every platform. Twitter, LinkedIn, Reddit, Hacker News — plays everywhere humans worry about being replaced.

The only meme that gets MORE true over time. Every headcount reduction re-triggers it. Every automation milestone re-triggers it. Built-in temporal compounding. The one people screenshot in October and say called it.

skippy.name: Skippy
skippy.personality: The judge who tells you your plan is garbage

mando.name: Mando
mando.personality: The bounty hunter who finds targets across any terrain

walle.name: WALL-E
walle.personality: The signal sorter who finds patterns in noise
